prediction:
FROM customers c
| JOIN orders o ON c.customer_id = o.customer_id AND o.status = 'paid'
| GROUP c.customer_id, c.customer_name
| AGG c.customer_id, c.customer_name, COUNT(o.order_id) AS paid_order_count
repair_actions: Removed the WHERE o.status = 'paid' filter and moved the status condition into the JOIN ON clause: ON c.customer_id = o.customer_id AND o.status = 'paid'.
Kept GROUP BY and AGG steps unchanged (grouping by customer_id and customer_name and counting matching orders) to minimize changes.

prediction:
FROM products p
| JOIN order_items oi ON p.product_id = oi.product_id
| JOIN orders o ON oi.order_id = o.order_id
| WHERE o.status = 'paid' AND p.category = 'furniture'
| AGG SUM(oi.quantity) AS total_quantity
repair_actions: Merged the two WHERE steps into a single WHERE combining both predicates: o.status = 'paid' AND p.category = 'furniture'.
Verified and preserved exact filter literal values ('paid', 'furniture') from the schema candidate values; no other changes made since JOIN and AGG were marked correct.

prediction:
FROM customers c
| JOIN orders o ON c.customer_id = o.customer_id
| JOIN order_items oi ON o.order_id = oi.order_id
| JOIN products p ON oi.product_id = p.product_id
| GROUP c.customer_id, c.customer_name
| AGG COUNT(DISTINCT p.category) AS category_count
| SELECT c.customer_id, c.customer_name, category_count
| ORDER category_count DESC
repair_actions: Moved non-aggregated columns (c.customer_id, c.customer_name) out of AGG into a SELECT step.
Changed AGG to contain only the aggregate expression: COUNT(DISTINCT p.category) AS category_count.
Kept existing JOINs unchanged (could not convert to LEFT JOIN because LEFT JOIN is not permitted by SQL+ constraints).
Retained GROUP BY c.customer_id, c.customer_name and ORDER BY category_count DESC per original intent.

| GROUP c.customer_id, c.customer_name
| AGG COUNT(DISTINCT p.category) AS category_count
| SELECT c.customer_id, c.customer_name, category_count
| ORDER category_count DESC","repair_actions":["Moved non-aggregated columns (c.customer_id, c.customer_name) out of AGG into a SELECT step.","Changed AGG to contain only the aggregate expression: COUNT(DISTINCT p.category) AS category_count.","Kept existing JOINs unchanged (could not convert to LEFT JOIN because LEFT JOIN is not permitted by SQL+ constraints).","Retained GROUP BY c.customer_id, c.customer_name and ORDER BY category_count DESC per original intent."],"confidence":"medium"}
